content.text:
## Application Under Test
- App name: SauceDemo
- Target URL: https://www.saucedemo.com/
- Environment: production
- Auth required: yes

## Feature Overview
Covers login behavior for valid, invalid, locked, and performance-sensitive users. This is the best-supported starter target for the agentic automation stack because the DOM is stable and credentials are known.

## Known UI Entry Points
- Login page: /
- Inventory: /inventory.html

## Test Types Required
- Functional
- Negative
- Boundary
- Cross-browser

## Automation Framework
- Primary: Playwright
- Language: TypeScript
- Script format: gherkin + playwright

story.summary: As a user, I want invalid and locked SauceDemo logins to show deterministic errors so that negative authentication is covered

content.text:
## Target URL
https://www.saucedemo.com/

## User Flow
1. Navigate to the SauceDemo login page
2. Enter invalid or locked credentials
3. Submit the login form
4. Verify the page remains on login and the Epic sadface error is visible

## DOM Selectors
- Username: #user-name
- Password: #password
- Submit: #login-button
- Error: [data-test='error']

## Test Data
- Locked: locked_out_user / secret_sauce
- Invalid: invalid_user / wrong_pass

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given locked credentials When login is submitted Then the locked-out error is displayed
- Given invalid credentials When login is submitted Then an invalid-credentials error is displayed

## Expected Outcomes
- User remains on the login page
- A deterministic error message is visible

## Out of Scope
- Password reset and SSO

data.content:
Page 1:
Project:  SCRUM -84 - SauceDemo Authentication Validation Functional Requirements:  
• R1: Persistent Login Page:  Any authentication failure MUST NOT redirect the user. 
The browser must remain on the root index.  
• R2: Epic Sadface Branding:  All error messages must be prepended with the string 
"Epic sadface:". Failure to include this branding indicates a UI regression.  
• R3: Lockout Protocol:  The system must distinguish between a "bad password" and 
a "locked account" to prevent brute -force confusion.  
 
The Script -Driven Execution Flow  
Now that your data is ready, here is how the BMAD Factory  handles it without manual 
intervention:  
1. Jira Sync (Phase 0):  python3 jira_sync_agent.py pulls the story text and these 3 
attachments into docs/inbox/.  
2. BMM Ingest (Phase 1):  vectorize_and_upload.py reads the PDF and CSVs. It 
creates a vector "Memory" that links the word "Locked"  to the specific error text in 
the CSV.  
3. Discovery (Phase 2):  Your generalized dom_capture.py hits SauceDemo. It finds 
#user -name and tags it as Input  because it matches the selectors.csv hint.  
4. TEA (Phase 3):  Playwright enters locked_out_user. It sees the error. It checks the 
Healer Guard —if the error container changed its ID, the agent queries Qdrant and 
says: "I found a new 'Feedback' element that contains the word 'Locked' from the 
FRD. This is the correct error."

SauceDemo negative authentication brief
Target URL: https://www.saucedemo.com/

Context
- Use the stable SauceDemo selectors only.
- Do not assert generic dashboard text; use URL and visible error banner.

data.expected_error_text: Sorry, this user has been locked out.
Username and password do not match any user in this service
